text.title: Embeddings Provider APIs — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `embeddings-provider-apis` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: All providers go behind a single `EmbeddingsClient.embed(texts, input_type)` interface. Direct provider calls rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a unified client, switching providers requires touching every call site.

rule.statement: Every call passes `input_type` (search_document / search_query / classification / clustering). Defaulting hidden behind silence rejected.
rule.rationale: input_type drives recall on asymmetric models; defaults make experiments non-reproducible.

rule.statement: Calls batch up to provider max; never one-text-at-a-time in production.
rule.rationale: Single-text calls multiply latency and burn rate-limit quota.

rule.statement: Client exposes `dims` parameter; on Matryoshka models the truncation is supported.
rule.rationale: Without truncation support, you can't experiment with storage / quality tradeoffs.

rule.statement: Client supports a fallback chain (provider A → provider B → local). Bare provider call rejected.
rule.rationale: Provider outages will happen; fallback chain protects retrieval availability.
